title: 250g coffee bag — laminate vs mono-PE vs paper vs compostable
summary: Material comparison: Virgin PET/AL/PE laminate | Mono PE/PE recyclable | Paper-based recyclable.
keyFinding: Paper coffee bags beat laminate on carbon by 19% — and on circularity by 5×.
context: Coffee packaging has high barrier requirements that traditionally favoured multilayer laminates. Modern paper structures with bio-coatings and paper valves now meet those requirements while delivering a kerbside-recyclable end-of-life pathway. The carbon win comes from recycling diversion; the circularity win comes from being recyclable in the first place.
primaryWhy: Coffee bag laminates are typically landfilled (85% in Aus). Paper-based bags follow kerbside recycling streams (68% recycled). That single difference accounts for most of the carbon delta.

title: Coffee SUP — virgin PE vs 30% PCR PE vs bio-PE
summary: Supply Chain comparison: Virgin PE/PE | PE/PE w 30% PCR | Bio-based PE/PE.
keyFinding: Feedstock choice swings carbon by 100% across otherwise-identical packs.
context: Pack design is held constant; only the feedstock varies for 250g coffee SUP. Carbon spans -914kg to 5,715kg CO2e per 100k units — a 100% spread on input choice alone. MCI moves modestly because the EOL pathway is largely shared; the cradle-side emissions do most of the work. Feedstock provenance and processing energy are the decisive variables.
primaryWhy: Pack design is held constant; only the feedstock varies. This section isolates how renewable inputs alter the carbon calculation.
